title: Which Methods Are Idempotent?
prompt_md: A method is **idempotent** if making the same request many times has the same effect on the server as making it once. Which statement is correct?
options: Only GET is idempotent; every other method changes state each time
POST is idempotent, but GET, PUT, and DELETE are not
GET, PUT, and DELETE are idempotent, but POST is not
explanation_md: GET (just reads), PUT (replaces a resource with the same payload — repeating lands on the same final state), and DELETE (deleting an already-deleted thing leaves it deleted) are all idempotent. POST is not: calling POST /users twice typically creates two users. Idempotency matters for safe retries — a client can resend an idempotent request after a network timeout without fear of duplicating work.
